disclaimer: NyayaLens provides legal information for education and issue spotting only. It is not legal advice and does not create a lawyer-client relationship.
demo_corpus_notice: The local corpus may contain simplified demo sources. Replace them with official legal sources before real-world use.

reasons: Unpaid payment issue for a contractor/freelancer based on a service agreement.

safety_result.reason: No unsafe user intent detected in active user text.

extracted_clauses.name: freelancer_role
extracted_clauses.value: Freelancer will provide project management services as per the scope of work
extracted_clauses.raw_text: Freelancer will provide project management services as per the scope of work
extracted_clauses.risk_hint: Role/category matters for payment route selection.
additional_occurrences: Freelancer as a Freelancer, hereinafter referred to as the Freelancer
Freelancer's designation will be Project Manager

extracted_clauses.name: consideration_clause
extracted_clauses.value: Consideration: Compensation details shall be paid for services rendered by the Freelancer
extracted_clauses.raw_text: Consideration: Compensation details shall be paid for services rendered by the Freelancer
additional_occurrences: Compensation details as per pro-rata compensation table

extracted_clauses.name: invoice_clause
extracted_clauses.value: monthly billing invoice generated 7 days prior to month end
extracted_clauses.raw_text: Invoice shall be generated for the monthly billing 7 days prior to the end of the month
extracted_clauses.risk_hint: Invoice timing can determine due date and evidence.

extracted_clauses.name: payment_timing_clause
extracted_clauses.value: last week of the pro-rata month
extracted_clauses.raw_text: Payment shall be made in the last week of the pro-rata data month after invoice approval
extracted_clauses.risk_hint: Payment timing can show when the amount became due.

extracted_clauses.name: compensation_clause
extracted_clauses.value: Compensation details shall be paid for services rendered by the Freelancer
extracted_clauses.raw_text: Compensation details shall be paid for services rendered by the Freelancer
extracted_clauses.risk_hint: Compensation table or amount should be compared with unpaid amount.
additional_occurrences: Compensation details as per pro-rata compensation table

extracted_clauses.name: pro_rata_compensation_clause
extracted_clauses.value: pro-rata data month after invoice approval
extracted_clauses.raw_text: pro-rata data month after invoice approval
extracted_clauses.risk_hint: Pro-rata wording should be compared with the actual work period.

extracted_clauses.name: tds_clause
extracted_clauses.raw_text: TDS will be deducted as per applicable
extracted_clauses.risk_hint: TDS wording may affect net payable amount.

extracted_clauses.name: independent_contractor_clause
extracted_clauses.risk_hint: Worker classification can affect remedy route.

extracted_clauses.name: termination_notice_clause
extracted_clauses.risk_hint: Termination notice can affect final payment timing.

extracted_clauses.name: termination_clause
extracted_clauses.value: terminate this Agreement by giving one month's written notice
extracted_clauses.raw_text: terminate this Agreement by giving one month's written notice
extracted_clauses.risk_hint: Termination wording can affect final payment or notice.

missing_facts: whether any partial payment was made
whether deduction is TDS, adjustment, penalty, or disputed withholding
whether the dispute is salary, stipend, invoice, consulting fee, freelance fee, or full-and-final settlement

expert_route.route_reason: Unpaid payment issue for a contractor/freelancer based on a service agreement.

citation.title: Employment Exit General Information
citation.excerpt:
Post-employment non-compete restrictions need careful legal review. Broad restrictions after employment may be difficult to enforce, but confidentiality and non-solicitation obligations can still matter depending on facts and contract wording.

Long notice periods should be checked against the signed contract, appointment letter, role category, company policy, and applicable state-specific employment law. If jurisdiction is unclear, confidence should be reduced.

citation.title: Employment Exit General Information
citation.excerpt:
DEMO CORPUS: This is a simplified educational placeholder. Replace with official legal sources before real-world use.

Employment Exit General Information

Employees should preserve offer letters, employment contracts, HR policies, resignation emails, payslips, attendance records, and written communication about final settlement. A dispute about unpaid wages or final settlement should be documented in writing with a request for calculation details.

Employment bonds and training cost recovery are contract issues. A fair assessment usually depends on the text of the agreement, proof of actua...

citation.title: Contract Clause General Information
citation.excerpt:
DEMO CORPUS: This is a simplified educational placeholder. Replace with official legal sources before real-world use.

Contract Clause General Information

Arbitration clauses may affect the forum or process for dispute resolution. Jurisdiction clauses may identify a city or court, but their effect depends on the contract and applicable law.

For deductions, recovery, or withholding clauses, ask for the written clause, itemized calculation, proof of loss or expense, and the legal basis for deduction. Avoid verbal-only communication and preserve copies of all notices and replies.

This educa...

rule_checks.title: Unpaid compensation/payment issue
evidence: plain_text_dispute_description: I have not been paid for the last invoice.
rule_checks.explanation: General information from deterministic checks: General information from deterministic checks. Unpaid wages, exit settlement, or contractor payments should be documented in writing with proof of agreed amount, work performed, due date, and prior payment follow-ups.
rule_checks.suggested_next_step: Preserve contract/offer letter/service agreement, payslips/invoices, bank statements, work proof, and written payment follow-ups.

rule_checks.title: Missing payment evidence
evidence: Payment terms were found, but the unpaid amount, invoice/payment request, work period, and payment follow-up evidence are still missing.
rule_checks.explanation: General information from deterministic checks: The system could not identify complete documents showing unpaid amount, due date, invoice/payment request, proof of work delivered, and written payment follow-ups.
rule_checks.suggested_next_step: Upload offer letter, contract/service agreement, payslip, invoice, attendance/work logs, work delivery proof, or payment emails.

rule_checks.title: Payment clause found; compare unpaid amount against agreement
evidence: Consideration: Compensation details shall be paid for services rendered by the Freelancer
Invoice shall be generated for the monthly billing 7 days prior to the end of the month
Payment shall be made in the last week of the pro-rata data month after invoice approval
Compensation details shall be paid for services rendered by the Freelancer
rule_checks.explanation: General information from deterministic checks: The uploaded document appears to contain payment, invoice, consideration, or compensation terms that should be compared with the unpaid amount and work period.
rule_checks.suggested_next_step: Match the unpaid amount against the invoice/payment clause, compensation table, work delivered, and due date.

rule_checks.title: TDS/deduction clarification needed
evidence: TDS will be deducted as per applicable
rule_checks.explanation: General information from deterministic checks: General information from deterministic checks. Tax, TDS, adjustment, penalty, or withholding wording should be separated from any disputed non-payment amount.
rule_checks.suggested_next_step: Ask for an itemized calculation showing whether the deduction is TDS, a contractual adjustment, a penalty, or disputed withholding.

rule_checks.title: Contractor/freelancer classification ambiguity
rule_checks.explanation: General information from deterministic checks: Different routes may apply depending on whether the person is an employee, contractor, intern, freelancer, or consultant.
rule_checks.suggested_next_step: Clarify whether payment is salary, stipend, full-and-final settlement, invoice amount, consulting fee, or freelance fee.

rule_checks.title: Jurisdiction-specific remedy route needed
rule_checks.explanation: General information from deterministic checks: Available routes may depend on state, worker category, contract terms, and whether the dispute is employment, labour, civil, or contract-related.
rule_checks.suggested_next_step: Confirm workplace/service location, contract jurisdiction, and payment due date.

rule_checks.title: Arbitration/jurisdiction clause may affect dispute path
rule_checks.explanation: General information from deterministic checks: Arbitration or jurisdiction wording may affect where or how a payment dispute is raised.
rule_checks.suggested_next_step: Preserve the forum clause and ask legal aid or a lawyer how it affects the next step.

risk_flags.title: Unpaid compensation/payment issue
triggering_evidence: plain_text_dispute_description: I have not been paid for the last invoice.
risk_flags.explanation: General information from deterministic checks: General information from deterministic checks. Unpaid wages, exit settlement, or contractor payments should be documented in writing with proof of agreed amount, work performed, due date, and prior payment follow-ups.
risk_flags.suggested_next_step: Preserve contract/offer letter/service agreement, payslips/invoices, bank statements, work proof, and written payment follow-ups.

THE SPECIFIC RELIEF ACT, 1963                                                                   
_______ 
ARRANGEMENT OF SECTIONS                                                                     
________ 
PART I 
PRELIMINARY 
SECTIONS 
1. Short title, extent and commencement. 
2. Definitions. 
3. Savings. 
4. Specific relief to be granted only for enforcing individual civil rights and not for enforcing penal 
laws. 
PART II 
SPECIFIC RELIEF 
CHAPTER I 
RECOVERING POSSESSION OF PROPERTY 
5. Recovery of specific immovable property. 
6. Suit by person dispossessed of immovable property.

potential_provision_matches.section_title: Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020
potential_provision_matches.source_quote:
THE OCCUPATIONAL SAFETY, HEALTH AND WORKING
CONDITIONS CODE, 2020
NO. 37 OF 2020
[28th September, 2020.]
An Act to consolidate and amend the laws regulating the occupational safety, health
and working conditions of the persons employed in an establishment and for
matters connected therewith or incidental thereto.
BE it enacted by Parliament in the Seventy-first Year of the Republic of India as
follows:—
CHAPTER I
PRELIMINARY
1. (1) This Act may be called the Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions
Code, 2020.
(2) It shall come into force on such date as the Central Government may, b
potential_provision_matches.why_relevant: Potentially relevant to worker-classification review; the system does not assume employee or contractor status.
missing_facts: whether payslips or invoices were issued

Code, 2020.

appoint; and different dates may be appointed for different provisions of this Code and any
reference in any such provision to the commencement of this Code shall be construed as a
reference to the coming into force of that provision.

application.

uncertainties: Only demo corpus material may have been retrieved; it is not a complete statement of Indian law.

steps: Preserve the service agreement, invoices, work delivery proof, emails, payment reminders, and bank records.
Ask the company/client for a written payment status.
Ask for an itemized calculation if any deduction or adjustment is proposed.
Clarify whether any deduction is TDS, contractual adjustment, penalty, or disputed withholding.
Avoid verbal-only communication; confirm calls in writing.
Clarify whether the amount is salary, invoice, stipend, consulting fee, or full-and-final settlement.
Consider legal aid or an appropriate civil/contract/labour route depending on worker classification and state.
Review the arbitration or dispute-resolution clause before escalation; ask legal aid or a lawyer how it affects the practical route.
evidence_checklist: Written reason for withholding payment, if any
remedy_plan.draft_message:
Subject: Request for written update on pending payment

Dear [Company/Client/Accounts Team],

I am writing to request a written update on the pending payment for my work/services. Please share the amount currently payable, the expected payment date, and any reason or document relied on for withholding, adjusting, or deducting any amount. I would also appreciate an itemized calculation if any deduction is proposed.

Regards,
[Your Name]
remedy_plan.escalation_note: The route may depend on whether this is an employment, contractor, consulting, freelance, or civil contract payment dispute.
